questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.We are discussing the project in the meeting right now.
options: The project in the meeting are discussing us now.
The project is being discussed in the meeting right now by us.
The project in the meeting were being discussed by us now
The project is being discussing in the meeting right now by us.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “The project is being discussed in the meeting right now by us”.The sentence is in present continuous tense. So, passive voice is also change according to it. Active voice ![](https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/website/bulk_uploader_images/1742023848321_image001.png) passive voice Subject / is/am/are + verb(Ing) + object object + is/am/are + being + V3 + by + subject Hence, “The project is being discussed in the meeting right now by us” is correct.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.A. Therefore, an advanced ambulance needs to reach the patient immediately. B. A cardiac arrest, as is well known, needs revival within 4 minutes. C. A question often asked by us in India is whether we will get timely health care in an emergency. D. But even in Delhi, the capital city, a patient does not get emergency care.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “CBAD”.A sentence can never be start with “but” and “therefore”. So, sentence will not start with (A) and (D). By making pair we get that A comes after B. So, BA is a pair. The correct sequence of the sentence formation is: C. A question often asked by us in India is whether we will get timely health care in an emergency. B. A cardiac arrest, as is well known, needs revival within 4 minutes. A. Therefore, an advanced ambulance needs to reach the patient immediately. D. But even in Delhi, the capital city, a patient does not get emergency care.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.Cloak-and-dagger
questions.explanation: the correct answer is “clandestine”.Cloak-and-dagger: involving or characterized by mystery, intrigue, or espionage. Bear with: be patient or tolerant with someone. Blow up: explode, lose one’s temper. Display: to show. Clandestine: kept secret or done secretively, especially because illicit.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the BOLD part of the given sentence.Don’t provoke Lisa, otherwise, she will hold a grudge against you.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Do not exasperate Lisa”.Provoke: make angry. Exasperate: it is synonym of provoke Equivocal: unclear and seeming to have two opposing meanings, or confusing. Hesitate: to pause before you do or say something.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the BOLD part of the given sentence.I find playing kabaddi to be difficult than playing cricket.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “to be most difficult”.In the given sentence there are comparison between two games (kabaddi and cricket). For comparison between two we use “more”. So, “to be more difficult” is correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the highlighted word.Seema action doesn’t breach such a punishment.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “violation”.Breach: an act of breaking of falling to observe a law, agreement, or code of conduct. Support: To agree with and give encouragement to someone or something because you want him, her, or it to succeed. Violation: Violation is often used to describe an action that disregards an agreement or a basic right, such as a violation of a global climate treaty or a human rights violation. Triumph: A great victory or achievement. Captivate: To hold the attention of someone by being extremely interesting, exciting, pleasant, or attractive.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the highlighted idiom.The leader was trying to twist the player’s arm by pressuring him to join the team.
options: To try to force someone to do something
To ask to join the team
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “To try to force someone to do something”.Twist the player’s arm: to force someone to do something by making it hard for them to refuse.

questions.question: Select the sentence which has no spelling error.
options: The classroom was filled with quiescence as the students focused on their exams.
The library was a haven of quiecsence, allowing me to concentrate on my reading undisturbed.
The city streets were filled with noise and chaos, but the park offered a moment of quiescense.
After a long day of hiking, I found solace in the peaceful queiscence of the forest.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “The classroom was filled with quiescence as the students focused on their exams”.Correct spelling is “quiescence” which is used to describe a state of calm, stillness, or tranquility.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Tolerant
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Tolerant”.Tolerant: open-minded, easy going. Fanatic: someone with very strong unreasonable beliefs. Humane: kind and compassionate. Liberal: progressive, broad-minded. Receptive: ready to listen to new ideas, suggestions, etc.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.In clover
options: Being in a state of confusion or chaos
Being in a state of luxury or prosperity
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Being in a state of luxury or prosperity”.In clover: A comfortable and financially privileged lifestyle.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.Someone who studies or writes about the meaning of life, knowledge or beliefs.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Philosopher”.Pervasive: existing in or spreading through every part of something. Pariah: A person who is not accepted by a social group. (outcast) Philosopher: Someone who studies or writes about the meaning of life. Philanthropist: someone who freely gives money and help to people who need it.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Deliberate
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “imprudent”.Deliberate: done consciously, and intentionally. Severe: strict in judgment, discipline, or government. Genuine: truly what something is said to be; authentic. Imprudent: not showing care for the consequences of an action. Honest: free of deceit; truthful and sincere.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.The team manager is examining the documents of all the players.
options: Examination of the documents of all the players was done by the team manager.
The documents of all the players are being examined by the team manager.
the documents of all the players the team manager is examining.
the team manager has been examining the documents of all players.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “The documents of all the players are being examined by the team manager”.Rule: Active voice ![](https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/website/bulk_uploader_images/1742023848321_image001.png) Passive voice Subject + is/am/are + verb (Ing) + object ![](https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/website/bulk_uploader_images/1742023848321_image002.png) Object + is/am/are + being + V3 + Subject Team manager is examining the documents of all the players The documents of all the players are being examined by the team manager.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.The sky is quite overcast. We’re ____________ to have rain today.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “likely”.Likely: it will probably happen or is expected. Mostly: as regards the greater part or number. Surely: used to express that you are certain or almost certain about something. Probably: to say that something is likely to happen.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.Menace
questions.explanation: Option d is the correct answer. Menace: something that is likely to cause harm. Safety: a state in which or a place where you are safe and not in danger or at risk. Auspicious: giving or being a sign of future success. Aid: help or support. Peril: serious and immediate danger.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.Apoorva loves pet.
options: Pet are being loved by Apoorva.
Pet will be loved by her.
Pet have been loved by Apoorva.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Pet is loved by Apoorva”.Rule: Active voice ![](https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/website/bulk_uploader_images/1742023848321_image001.png) Passive voice Subject + V1 + object object + is/am/are + V3 + by + subject Apoorva loves pet Pet is loved by Apoorva

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “obsequis”.Obsequies: funeral rites. Humorous: causing laughter and amusement; comic. Mysterious: difficult or impossible to understand, explain, or identify. Scissors: A device used for cutting materials such as paper, cloth, and hair.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the BOLD segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’.I am waiting for the Bus for the last one hours.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “I have been waiting”.Present perfect continuous tense will be use in this sentence. When an action already started and still going on comes under present perfect continuous tense. Rule: subject + has/have + been +V (Ing) + object + for/since + time. Therefore, I have been waiting for the bus for the last one hour.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.Genial
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “amiable”.Genial: extremely nice and happy. Amiable: having or displaying a friendly and pleasant manner. Tedious: not interesting, boring, and monotonous. Boisterous: noisy and full of life and energy. Beneficiary: a person or group that receives benefits, profits, or advantages.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.Using other’s ideas without permission
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Plagiarism”.Plagiarism: The practice of taking someone else’s work or ideas and passing them off as one’s own. Kleptomaniac: Someone with a very strong wish to steal that they cannot control. Dadaism: Going against tradition. Parochialism: the quality of showing interest only in a narrow range of matters, especially those that directly affect yourself, your town, or your country.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.She was flying over the hills and felt delighted at the (1) ______ of the tall trees and their (2) ______ branches below her. She could (3) ______ see a river flowing in a zig-zag fashion. Birds (4) ______ alongside making chirruping sounds as if (5) ______ her to the skies. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “sight”.Sight refers to the act of seeing or the ability to see. The sentence describes the person flying over the hills and feeling delighted at the sight of the tall trees and their branches below her. Therefore, sight is the most appropriate answer.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.She was flying over the hills and felt delighted at the (1) ______ of the tall trees and their (2) ______ branches below her. She could (3) ______ see a river flowing in a zig-zag fashion. Birds (4) ______ alongside making chirruping sounds as if (5) ______ her to the skies. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “sloping”.The word “sloping” accurately describes the branches of the tall trees. When something is sloping, it means that it has a gentle incline or is at an angle. In this context, the sentence is conveying that the person flying over the hills felt delighted at the sight of the tall trees and their branches below her. By using the word “sloping,” it suggests that the branches are extending in a slanted or inclined manner.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.She was flying over the hills and felt delighted at the (1) ______ of the tall trees and their (2) ______ branches below her. She could (3) ______ see a river flowing in a zig-zag fashion. Birds (4) ______ alongside making chirruping sounds as if (5) ______ her to the skies. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “even”.The word “even” fits well in the sentence context. Even means used to emphasize something surprising or extreme.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.She was flying over the hills and felt delighted at the (1) ______ of the tall trees and their (2) ______ branches below her. She could (3) ______ see a river flowing in a zig-zag fashion. Birds (4) ______ alongside making chirruping sounds as if (5) ______ her to the skies. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “flew”.In the given sentence past participle form of fly will be used because the given passage is from the third person viewpoint.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.She was flying over the hills and felt delighted at the (1) ______ of the tall trees and their (2) ______ branches below her. She could (3) ______ see a river flowing in a zig-zag fashion. Birds (4) ______ alongside making chirruping sounds as if (5) ______ her to the skies. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “welcoming”.The sentence describes birds flying alongside the person who is flying over the hills and making chirruping sounds. The phrase “as if welcoming her to the skies” suggests that the birds’ chirruping sounds are giving a sense of warmth and friendliness, making the person feel welcomed.
